sizing_rule.note: Ralph Vince optimal f method: position size = Account Equity / (Worst Case Loss Per Contract / divisor). Baseline divisor 0.1 equates to 1 contract per $10,000 equity. Formula: Contracts = Account Equity / (Max Loss Per Contract × 10). Author does not specify explicit optimal_f fraction but implies optimal sizing via loss-to-equity ratio.

source_rule_text: Position size is determined by dividing account equity by the worst-case loss per contract, with one contract per $10,000 in equity serving as the baseline.
